title: Effect of three-party acknowledgment; alternative to disavowal; child support order
doc_html:
§402.1. Effect of three-party acknowledgment; alternative to disavowal; child support order

            The execution of a three-party acknowledgment pursuant to Civil Code Article 190.1 terminates the obligation to pay child support by the husband or former husband and revokes any court order enforcing that obligation. However, it does not affect any child support payment or arrearages paid, due, or owing prior to the date the three-party acknowledgment was executed.

            Acts 2020, No. 266, §1.
doc_text:
§402.1.  Effect of three-party acknowledgment; alternative to disavowal; child support order
The execution of a three-party acknowledgment pursuant to Civil Code Article 190.1
terminates the obligation to pay child support by the husband or former husband and revokes
any court order enforcing that obligation.  However, it does not affect any child support
payment or arrearages paid, due, or owing prior to the date the three-party acknowledgment
was executed.
Acts 2020, No. 266, §1.
